title: Cleome bungei
intro: Cleome bungei, also known as Bunge's spider flower, is a perennial plant native to Central Asia and China, characterized by its showy, pink or white flowers and hairy, branching stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cleome bungei include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleome bungei is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in combination with other herbs in teas, tinctures, and salves to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Cleome bungei is classified as a species within the family Cleomaceae. This family, Cleomaceae, is a part of the larger clade Brassicales. The plant belongs to the order Brassicales, which is a group of flowering plants. Cleome bungei is a member of the genus Cleome, which contains around 80-100 species. The classification of Cleome bungei reflects its relationship to other flowering plants within the Brassicales clade.
uses: Cleome bungei, also known as the rose mallow or bee plant, is said to have various medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help in treating wounds and skin infections. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cleome bungei for medicinal purposes.
